text.title: FinOps for AI / ML Workloads Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: GPU training MUST use spot/preemptible instances when (a) job supports checkpointing every ≤30 min and (b) target interruption rate ≤5%. Otherwise on-demand.
rule.rationale: Spot delivers 60-90% savings but is unsafe without checkpoints; non-checkpointable jobs lose more in re-training than they save.

rule.statement: Inference endpoints MUST cache deterministic / near-deterministic responses (embeddings, classification, system-prompt-bound classification) with TTL aligned to model version lifecycle.
rule.rationale: Repeat-input rate of 20-40% is typical in production; caching trims both cost and latency.

rule.statement: New model deployments MUST evaluate int8/int4 quantization first; full-precision deployments require evidence that quality regresses beyond the SLA threshold.
rule.rationale: Modern quantization holds quality within 1-3% on most tasks while halving cost; skipping evaluation is leaving money on the table.

rule.statement: Batchable inference workloads (offline scoring, embeddings ingestion, async tasks) MUST use dynamic batching with target batch size matching the model's optimal throughput curve.
rule.rationale: Single-request inference underutilizes GPU; dynamic batching can 3-10x throughput on the same hardware.

rule.statement: Every training run MUST emit a cost record with job_id, team, dataset_hash, gpu_hours, total_cost. Every inference endpoint MUST emit per-team cost tags.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, FinOps reviews devolve into shared blame; chargeback puts the spend conversation with the team that drives it.

rule.statement: Skip when GPU spend <$2k/month AND inference spend <$1k/month — vendor SKU optimization wins; OR no checkpointing primitive available; OR latency-critical inference incompatible with cache TTL.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the engineering overhead exceeds savings; above threshold prerequisites are non-negotiable.
